claim_boundary: Each row is a mandatory regression and owner handoff. It is not proof until the named current test and parent evidence gate pass.

rows.origin: historical live run packet-0205/result-0209/event-5616
rows.owner: control-plane ledger hygiene runtime and historical replay
rows.expected_reaction: mechanical reject with no dirty accepted pointer or terminal continuation

rows.origin: 13 base families registered 122 forbidden paths while responder projected zero
rows.owner: ContractExhaustionMesh and strict fake-AI responder
rows.expected_reaction: all registered forbidden paths receive stable cells and no vacuous green is allowed

rows.origin: fake responses could use body mechanical mirrors instead of the opened checklist
rows.owner: current handoff/checklist single authority
rows.expected_reaction: fail closed; body content cannot repair, override, or reconstruct the checklist

rows.origin: fake Reviewer could reconstruct review depth from static flow id
rows.owner: delivered review_window and contract fingerprint
rows.expected_reaction: missing, unknown, or tampered delivered review policy is rejected

rows.origin: unknown, planner, project_manager, or wrong-role values could normalize to Worker or continue
rows.owner: host, role handoff, runtime responsibility normalization, and current Cartesian
rows.expected_reaction: mechanical reject before dispatch/open/submit continuation

rows.origin: daemon_replay appeared as a positive current execution source
rows.owner: current-contract source-purity Cartesian
rows.expected_reaction: historical negative only; no current stage can continue

rows.origin: public fake E2E found active acc-003 absent from a static route-plan minimum, causing endless mechanical reissue after the model was green
rows.owner: runtime dynamic PM repair contract projection and Model-Miss Review
rows.expected_reaction: all current active acceptance ids are projected to a route owner and supplemental repair item before submit

rows.origin: ordinary json.loads silently keeps the last duplicate key, so malformed duplicate-key intent can look valid
rows.owner: formal deterministic fuzz and public submit boundary
rows.expected_reaction: duplicate-aware fuzz detects the source ambiguity and records the observed public boundary instead of pretending ordinary parsing caught it

rows.origin: layered boundary cells copied declared owner, reaction, and current status into observed outputs, making equality vacuous
rows.owner: FlowGuard layered boundary proof artifact projection
rows.expected_reaction: observed outputs come from current result fields and missing, stale, or tampered proof artifacts make the parent non-passing

rows.origin: the evidence compiler previously attached the compile-time source fingerprint without proving the background tier started and ended on that source
rows.owner: test-tier supervisor and TestMesh evidence compiler
rows.expected_reaction: supervisor records start/end fingerprints, source change fails the tier, and compiler rejects any missing or mismatched fingerprint chain

rows.origin: the broad synthetic-agent matrix reused declared test names and generated cells as passed/current after MTA correctly marked them not_run
rows.owner: synthetic coverage declaration boundary, formal AI execution closure, and PPA Risk Evidence Ledger
rows.expected_reaction: the broad matrix reports declaration_only with zero executed/passed/proof-backed counts, while PPA consumes current formal AI and strict MTA evidence instead of a hard-coded green

rows.origin: the installed shadow-launcher test passed but the outer tier owner terminated three exact Windows descendants still completing normal teardown after the former two-second window
rows.owner: background test child exact-process settlement and descendant-zero proof
rows.expected_reaction: an exact descendant may exit naturally within the fifteen-second bounded window; any descendant surviving that window is terminated and the child receipt remains failed

rows.origin: a release owner launched from frozen FlowGuard 0.58.0 but its nested literal python resolved to the concurrently updated default FlowGuard 0.58.1 environment
rows.owner: background command execution-owner toolchain binding
rows.expected_reaction: an exact leading python token is bound to the current execution-owner interpreter for both launch and receipt verification; external toolchain drift cannot enter the frozen release plan

rows.origin: the Windows virtual-environment launcher exited before its real packaged Python child completed a passing shadow-launcher pytest command, causing the outer owner to mistake active command execution for orphan settlement
rows.owner: background child direct interpreter process-owner binding
rows.expected_reaction: the frozen base interpreter is the direct tracked process owner while __PYVENV_LAUNCHER__ preserves the requested virtual-environment identity; bounded settlement remains reserved for descendants after the real command exits

rows.origin: initial analysis of the surviving daemon exposed that stop cleanup only sampled descendants before the wait; later command evidence established startup write-lock failure as the primary repeated incident
rows.owner: Router daemon exact-tree stop transaction
rows.expected_reaction: the current stop transaction accumulates exact descendants throughout the wait, cleans every surviving identity, and releases the lock only after descendant-zero confirmation

rows.origin: under the real two-owner all-tier load, start failed with PermissionError while foreground startup and the newly opened daemon contended for startup_state.json.write.lock; the test then could not enter its cleanup finally block
rows.owner: current runtime JSON write-lock acquisition and existing foreground writer settlement
rows.expected_reaction: transient Windows permission denial stays inside the existing bounded writer settlement and uses the same lock path; persistent denial becomes structured RouterLedgerWriteInProgress and blocks

rows.origin: after write-lock permission entered settlement, one start command retried new_invocation and allocated three run roots with separate live daemons; after single allocation, the final retry initially lost actions completed before contention
rows.owner: public start single allocation, foreground writer settlement, and exact current-run daemon startup authority
rows.expected_reaction: one start allocates one persisted bootstrap, retries advance that bootstrap, reattaches one exact live in-flight daemon, preserves completed folded-action evidence, and rejects multiple live startup owners

rows.origin: strict MTA entered the run_until_wait writer-contention branch and exposed that the split module referenced RouterLedgerWriteInProgress without importing it
rows.owner: run_until_wait current exception handler and foreground settlement receipt chain
rows.expected_reaction: the current writer-contention exception is caught by its real type, carries only completed folded actions, and is merged by the existing foreground settlement owner without an alternate retry path

rows.origin: the final ModelMesh correctly blocked because the verification contract refreshed strict MTA but did not require the behavior-commitment and primary-path risk result to consume the current formal AI execution and MTA results first
rows.owner: OpenSpec verification receipt dependency chain
rows.expected_reaction: the single release sequence runs formal AI execution, strict MTA, behavior-commitment risk, and ModelMesh in that order; a missing or stale intermediate result blocks rather than falling back to an aggregate tier result

rows.origin: the required known-bad suite expected the repository's live unified-repair result to remain red, so the test failed after exact native evidence legitimately made that shared result green
rows.owner: ModelMesh unified-repair negative test fixture
rows.expected_reaction: the negative test creates a source-matching red unified-repair result inside its own temporary project root and proves the always-on gate consumes it without depending on mutable repository evidence
